channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Xbox Console Prices Raising By $100+, 2TB Model Sunsetting
item.content:encoded:
If the recently announced cost of the Steam Machines gobsmacked you, well Microsoft has decided to make its living room entertainment offerings more expensive in the near future. The Xbox Series X and S have their price getting hiked by a minimum of $100 depending on the memory of the device.

Interestingly, Microsoft is not making differentiation between the Xbox Series X and the Xbox Series S for how much the price is being increased, as the price increases coming in effect August 1 follow a simple pattern based on the size of the SSD.

  • 512 GB Models: $100 price increase
  • 1TB Models: $150 price increase
  • 2TB Models: Retired

This is the third announced price increase in the last 13 months for the Xbox Series consoles. In May 2025, they raised the price of $380 or $430 for Xbox Series S versions of 512 GB and 1 TB sizes respectively, while Xbox Series X went to $550 for the digital version and $600 for the version with a disc drive. Then effective October 3 last Fall, they raised the price another $20 for Xbox Series S, while raising the Xbox Series X All Digital to $599, the version with a digital drive to $649, and the 2TB Xbox Series X version to $799.

At the same time, we've also seen the price of Xbox Game Pass increase significantly, even after a price adjustment back in April.

In explaining their decision Microsoft states that, 'console storage and memory prices have increased by more than 2.5x and we expect another doubling by the fall of 2027', which is largely due to AI data centers and spending there, which Microsoft is one of the companies helping push. They also plead that consoles are typically sold at a loss unlike many other consumer devices so presumably cannot afford to eat more of the cost of the current situation that they have helped create.

They also state that they are making it easier for users to go into debt to buy Xbox Series X|S devices, using 'Buy Now, Pay Later', and Interest Free Financing for up to 12 months. Additionally they are trying to encourage retailers with new programs on trading in used consoles, and on their certified refurbished consoles, for which you'll be able to buy a used, repaired console at the price you could currently get a shiny new one.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don
